name: Archuleta Mesa
description: Archuleta Mesa, located near Dulce, New Mexico, is the focal point of a controversial conspiracy theory alleging a vast, secret underground facility used for genetic experiments, collaboration with extraterrestrial beings, and other clandestine activities. The theory gained prominence in the late 1980s and early 1990s, fueled by purported leaked documents, anecdotal accounts from individuals claiming to have worked at the facility, and UFO sightings in the area. Proponents claim the facility, often referred to as the 'Dulce Base,' is a multi-level complex housing advanced technology and serving as a hub for human-alien interaction. Evidence cited includes purported blueprints, whistleblower testimonies detailing alien encounters and genetic engineering projects, and geological anomalies detected in the mesa. Mainstream scientific and government sources dismiss these claims as unfounded, citing a lack of credible evidence and attributing reported anomalies to natural geological formations or misinterpretations of legitimate research activities conducted in the region. The area around Dulce has a history of military activity and Native American lore, contributing to the enduring appeal of the conspiracy.
subtitle: Alleged underground alien research facility.

claims.claim: There is a secret underground base beneath Archuleta Mesa.
claims.evidence: Whistleblower testimonies, purported blueprints, geological anomalies.
claims.criticism: Lack of verifiable evidence, inconsistencies in testimonies, alternative explanations for geological findings.

claims.claim: The base is used for genetic experiments involving humans and aliens.
claims.evidence: Alleged leaked documents detailing genetic manipulation, claims of hybrid beings.
claims.criticism: Documents are of questionable authenticity, lack scientific corroboration, claims are based on anecdotal evidence.

claims.claim: The base is a joint operation between the US government and extraterrestrial beings.
claims.evidence: Claims of alien sightings, alleged government cover-ups, testimonies of former employees.
claims.criticism: Sightings are often misidentified objects or natural phenomena, government denials, lack of concrete proof of collaboration.

keyFigures.name: Paul Bennewitz
keyFigures.role: Early researcher and promoter of the Dulce Base theory.

keyFigures.name: Phil Schneider
keyFigures.role: Claimed to be a former government employee who worked at the Dulce Base.

keyFigures.name: Greg Bishop
keyFigures.role: Author and researcher who has investigated the Dulce Base claims, often taking a critical perspective.

mythologyConnections: Native American stories of underground beings
Legends of portals to other dimensions

timeline.event: Paul Bennewitz begins investigating alleged UFO activity near Dulce.
The Dulce Base conspiracy theory gains widespread attention.
The Dulce Base theory continues to circulate in UFO and conspiracy communities.

sources.author: Greg Bishop
sources.work: Project Beta: The Story of Paul Bennewitz, National Security, and the Creation of a Modern Myth
sources.title: Greg Bishop
